description: Locks every currently completed foundational component: reproducible, hash-pinned, regression-tested, fail-closed, explicitly classified by what it does and does not prove, and required by the foundation-lock CI job. NO component here is promotion-eligible; none proves a market edge.

items.title: Market-superiority evaluator
invariants: Selection uses only the selection split; proof uses only the test split.
Date-clustered bootstrap; requires >= 2 clusters; deterministic under a fixed seed.
Signs: challenger-minus-market; logloss/Brier negative is better, AUC positive is better.
Pushes (actual == line) are excluded from binary metrics.
PASS requires ci bound past zero AND Holm-adjusted p <= alpha AND n >= min_rows.
Fail-closed: missing required columns raise; multiple candidates require a frozen map in prove mode.
limitations: The synthetic --self-test validates evaluator MECHANICS only; it is NOT real market evidence.
A PASS is evidence only for the exact frozen candidate, book, timestamps, dates, and population.

items.title: G0 baseline proof (historical, pre-Phase-0)
invariants: all_props_pass is false; no primary prop is recorded as PASS.
JSON, CSV, and Markdown renderings agree on per-prop n and gate.
RUN_MANIFEST per-prop counts and date ranges match the machine-readable proof.
Output artifact hashes are pinned; any change breaks verification.
limitations: Push-safe integer-line handling, exact quote identity, and evaluated-versus-deployed parity are NOT yet fixed.
Books were averaged in the input, so exact quote identity was not preserved.

items.title: Signed-CLV gate
invariants: CLV is signed and directional for the selected side (close minus open no-vig).
No max(|edge_over|,|edge_under|) nonnegative fallback is permitted.
Negative signed CLV cannot pass; missing closing quotes fail closed.
Ambiguous/many-to-many quote joins fail closed (duplicated-subset guard).
Confidence intervals are clustered by game date; insufficient clusters cannot pass.
limitations: Requires post-game closing quotes to evaluate; without them the gate fail-closes (skips), it does not pass.
CLV thresholds are unchanged by Foundation Lock v1.

items.title: Feature-ablation maps
invariants: check regenerates the resolved map and compares canonicalized JSON byte-for-byte.
G0 equals the full current feature contract for every direct prop.
Unknown or forbidden features are a hard error; duplicates are rejected.
Resolution is deterministic (stable ordering).
limitations: Resolves feature lists only; it does not train models or prove anything about performance.

items.title: Tracking/hustle collector (infrastructure only)
invariants: Output filenames, season range, and league id match the versioned schema contract.
Player frame selection keeps the id-bearing frame with the most columns; empty responses skipped.
GAME_ID is injected as string; player id column (personId or PLAYER_ID) is retained.
Duplicate (GAME_ID, player) rows are de-duplicated; the collector is resumable.
limitations: Collector code only. Tracking DATA is NOT landed and tracking FEATURES are NOT built.
Runs on a LOCAL residential IP only; never in CI/agent VMs (datacenter IPs are blocked).
This is not Phase B; it proves nothing about discrimination or market edge.

items.title: Current feature-matrix snapshot
invariants: Snapshot is self-consistent (schema_hash == hash(ordered_schema)) and pins the feature-contract hash.
Verifier detects parquet drift (sha/rows/cols/schema/date-range/duplicate-key).
A changed matrix requires snapshot regeneration or a new versioned snapshot; silent replacement is rejected.
Missing parquet fail-closes under --require-parquet; otherwise reports DEFERRED (not a silent skip).
limitations: The wide parquet is git-ignored (large, pipeline-built); the live-parquet drift check is deferred in clean CI checkouts.
A snapshot describes the matrix; it does not validate model performance.

items.title: Prop-ablation runner and verdict (corrected, surrogate/dev-only)
invariants: Classification is 'surrogate feature-subset ablation'; no final holdout is scored.
Expanding chronological development folds; reserved tail dates never enter any fold.
Deterministic ablation_verdict.json under fixed seed; JSON and Markdown generated by the runner.
Each candidate uses exactly its mapped feature list; results are not promotion-eligible.
limitations: Surrogate model (HGB Poisson -> NB PMF), NOT the signed champion OOF/PMF/calibration/delivery pipeline.
Development-only exploration; conditional-on-played (DNP excluded). NOT proof, certification, or promotion evidence.
Conclusion: no tested subset improved the surrogate under this protocol; tracking is a high-priority next source, not proven to be the only one.
